name: Shah Rukh Khan
description: Actor, producer — among India's most recognised global figures

stances.summary: Said there was 'extreme intolerance' in India and 'religious extremism and negativity' were making people 'scared'; faced massive backlash and boycott calls.
stances.quote: Religious extremism and intolerance is being taken to a new level... I sometimes feel scared.

stances.summary: No public statement found during the nationwide CAA-NRC protests.
No public statement found during the year-long farmers' agitation.
No public statement found on the ethnic violence in Manipur.

name: Virat Kohli
description: Former India cricket captain — one of the most followed Indians globally

stances.summary: Expressed support for the Indian armed forces after the Pulwama attack and Balakot airstrike.
No public statement found during the CAA-NRC protests.
No public statement found during the farmers' agitation.
No public statement found on ethnic violence in Manipur.
No public statement found when Olympic-medal-winning wrestlers protested against BJP MP Brij Bhushan Singh.

name: Sachin Tendulkar
description: Former cricketer, Rajya Sabha MP (nominated), Bharat Ratna recipient

stances.summary: Tweeted 'India's sovereignty cannot be compromised... Let's remain united as a nation' after Rihanna's tweet, using the #IndiaTogether and #IndiaAgainstPropaganda hashtags aligned with the government's messaging.
stances.quote: India's sovereignty cannot be compromised. External forces can be spectators but not participants... Let's remain united as a nation. #IndiaTogether #IndiaAgainstPropaganda

stances.summary: No public statement found.
No public statement found.

name: Aamir Khan
description: Actor, filmmaker — known for socially conscious cinema and Satyamev Jayate

stances.summary: Said his wife Kiran Rao had suggested leaving India due to growing intolerance. Faced severe backlash, boycott campaigns, and lost brand endorsements.
stances.quote: Kiran and I have lived all our lives in India. For the first time, she said, should we move out of India?

stances.summary: No public statement found during CAA protests despite earlier intolerance remarks.
No public statement found.

name: Amitabh Bachchan
description: Actor — among the most influential figures in Indian cinema history

stances.summary: Did not make any public statement during the intolerance debate despite being asked repeatedly.
No public statement found.
No public statement found.
No public statement found.

name: Akshay Kumar
description: Actor — conducted a non-political interview with PM Modi before 2019 elections

stances.summary: Conducted a widely criticised 'non-political' interview with PM Modi before the 2019 general elections, asking about mangoes and childhood rather than policy.
Tweeted in support of CAA: 'Proud of the government for not letting go of the persecuted minorities from our neighbouring countries.'
No public statement found during the farmers' agitation.
No public statement found.

name: Kangana Ranaut
description: Actor turned BJP MP — elected to Lok Sabha from Mandi, Himachal Pradesh in 2024

stances.summary: Called protesting farmers 'terrorists' and Khalistanis on social media. Had her Twitter account permanently suspended for repeated violations.
Publicly supported CAA and criticised protesters, called Shaheen Bagh protesters 'anti-national'.
No public statement condemning the ethnic violence despite being a BJP-aligned public figure. Later faced controversy for a slap on her security detail.
Criticised the protesting wrestlers and questioned their motives.

name: Deepika Padukone
description: Actor — one of the highest-paid actors in Indian cinema

stances.summary: Visited JNU on 7 January 2020 to stand in solidarity with students attacked by masked assailants. Did not make a speech but her presence was widely seen as a statement. Faced massive boycott campaigns against her film Chhapaak.
No public statement found.
No public statement found.

name: Priyanka Chopra Jonas
description: Actor, UN Goodwill Ambassador — one of India's most globally recognised figures

stances.summary: Tweeted 'Jai Hind #IndianArmedForces' after the Balakot airstrike. Later confronted at a public event by a Pakistani woman who called her a 'hypocrite' for supporting military action while being a UNICEF peace ambassador.
Liked an Instagram post supporting CAA protests but did not make a direct statement.
No public statement found.

name: Salman Khan
description: Actor — one of the highest-grossing Hindi film stars

stances.summary: Did not participate in the intolerance debate.
No public statement found.
No public statement found.
No public statement found.

name: Ajay Devgn
description: Actor, filmmaker

stances.summary: Tweeted 'Don't fall for any false propaganda against India or Indian policies' with #IndiaTogether and #IndiaAgainstPropaganda after Rihanna's tweet, echoing government messaging.
stances.quote: Don't fall for any satisfying propaganda against India or Indian policies. #IndiaTogether #IndiaAgainstPropaganda

stances.summary: No public statement found.

name: Anupam Kher
description: Actor — outspoken supporter of BJP and PM Modi

stances.summary: Led a counter-march titled 'March for India' against the intolerance narrative. Called returning-award writers 'anti-national'.
Actively campaigned in support of CAA, visited protest sites to counter anti-CAA protesters.
Criticised the farmers' protests and questioned the protesters' motives.
No public statement found condemning the ethnic violence in Manipur.

name: Swara Bhasker
description: Actor — one of the most consistently vocal critics of BJP policies

stances.summary: Participated in multiple anti-CAA protests including at Shaheen Bagh. Faced sustained online abuse and boycott campaigns.
Openly supported the farmers' protests, visited protest sites at Delhi borders.
Condemned the ethnic violence and criticised PM Modi's initial silence on the viral sexual assault video.
Spoke out in support of protesting wrestlers against BJP MP Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh.
Condemned the gang rape and the UP government's handling of the case, including the midnight cremation.

name: Naseeruddin Shah
description: Veteran actor — National Award winner and Padma Bhushan recipient

stances.summary: Spoke about growing atmosphere of intolerance and fear in India.
Released a video expressing concern about mob violence and the Bulandshahr cow-related violence. Said he feared for his children's safety.
stances.quote: In many areas we are witnessing that the weights of a cow is more than that of a police officer's.

stances.summary: Criticised CAA and spoke against the government's response to student protesters.

name: Javed Akhtar
description: Poet, lyricist, screenwriter — Padma Shri, Padma Bhushan recipient

stances.summary: Spoke against rising intolerance and compared RSS to the Taliban.
Criticised the Citizenship Amendment Act and supported the student protests.
Condemned the ethnic violence in Manipur and criticised PM Modi's silence.

name: Anurag Kashyap
description: Filmmaker — returned his National Award in protest against intolerance

stances.summary: Returned his National Award over rising intolerance and the Dadri lynching. One of the most prominent voices in the 'award wapsi' movement.
Actively criticised CAA on social media and called the government authoritarian.
Criticised the communication blackout in Kashmir after Article 370 abrogation.

name: Farhan Akhtar
description: Actor, filmmaker, singer — co-founded MARD foundation against gender violence

stances.summary: Participated in an anti-CAA rally at Mumbai's August Kranti Maidan. One of the few A-list Bollywood figures to physically attend a protest.
stances.quote: Every person, be it a man or woman, has the right to choose who they worship, what they eat and what they wear.

stances.summary: Condemned the violence and the viral video of two women being paraded naked.

name: Prakash Raj
description: Actor — contested 2019 Lok Sabha elections as an independent against BJP

stances.summary: Was among the most vocal critics. Criticised the government persistently on social media.
Participated in anti-CAA protests and criticised the government.
Supported farmers' protests and visited protest sites.
Condemned the violence and called out the PM's silence.

name: Vishal Dadlani
description: Music composer, singer — known for vocal political commentary

stances.summary: Actively spoke out against CAA on social media and supported the student protests.
Expressed solidarity with protesting farmers on social media.

name: Taapsee Pannu
description: Actor — known for her roles in socially relevant films

stances.summary: Tweeted support for farmers using the hashtag #FarmersProtest. Faced IT raids on her properties which were widely seen as retaliatory.
Expressed concern about CAA on social media.

name: Dia Mirza
description: Actor, UN Environment Goodwill Ambassador

stances.summary: Participated in protests against CAA and expressed solidarity with students at Jamia Millia Islamia.
Expressed support for farmers' rights.

name: Konkona Sen Sharma
description: Actor, filmmaker — National Award winner

stances.summary: Participated in anti-CAA protests in Mumbai and signed an open letter by filmmakers against CAA.
Condemned the violence on social media.

name: Richa Chadha
description: Actor — vocal on social issues

stances.summary: Spoke out against CAA and participated in protests.
Tweeted 'Galwan says hi' which was interpreted as mocking the Indian soldiers killed in the 2020 Galwan clash. Apologised and deleted the tweet.

name: Vivek Agnihotri
description: Filmmaker — directed The Kashmir Files, a government-endorsed film on Kashmiri Pandits

stances.summary: Directed The Kashmir Files (2022) depicting the exodus of Kashmiri Pandits. The film was actively promoted by PM Modi and BJP leaders, made tax-free in BJP-ruled states.
Actively supported CAA and criticised anti-CAA protesters.
No public statement found condemning the ethnic violence.

name: Sonam Kapoor
description: Actor

stances.summary: Tweeted condemning the attack on JNU students. Called it 'horrifying'.

name: Mukesh Ambani
description: Chairman of Reliance Industries — India's richest person

stances.summary: Praised demonetisation and said it would bring benefits to the Indian economy. Reliance Jio's digital payments push was seen as benefiting from the cash crunch.
No public statement found. Reliance towers were targeted by protesting farmers who saw corporate agriculture as a threat. Ambani did not comment publicly.
No public statement found.

name: Gautam Adani
description: Chairman of Adani Group — business ties with government extensively documented

stances.summary: Called the Hindenburg short-seller report a 'calculated attack on India' and framed it as an attack on the nation rather than on his business. Opposition demands for a JPC probe were rejected.
No public statement found.
No public statement found. Adani-owned grain storage facilities were targeted by protesting farmers.

name: Ratan Tata
description: Chairman Emeritus of Tata Sons (d. 2024) — India's most respected industrialist

stances.summary: Publicly supported demonetisation, calling it a 'bold and visionary step'.
Expressed concern about the violence during protests but did not take a clear stance on the law itself.
No public statement found.

name: Rihanna
description: Singer, businesswoman — one of the world's most followed people on social media

stances.summary: Tweeted a CNN article about the internet shutdown at farmer protest sites with the comment 'why aren't we talking about this?!' to her 100M+ followers. Triggered a massive international spotlight and a coordinated government/celebrity counter-response.
stances.quote: why aren't we talking about this?! #FarmersProtest

name: Greta Thunberg
description: Climate activist

stances.summary: Shared a 'toolkit' document to support the farmers' protests. Indian police filed an FIR over the toolkit, and activist Disha Ravi was arrested in connection.
stances.quote: Here's an updated toolkit by people on the ground in India if you want to help.

name: Meena Harris
description: Lawyer, author — niece of US Vice President Kamala Harris

stances.summary: Tweeted support for the farmers' protests, calling them 'the largest protest in human history'. The Indian government objected to foreign commentary on internal matters.
